english: take the measure, the dimensions of, to survey; sa (sa) land, nor (nor) the property, to take an inventory, to ascertain or compute the state of one's property, 3. C. = the following; nyams sád-pa (nyams sad pa) ccg. 1. to try, to test, byéd-dam mi byed (byed dam mi byed) whether he will do it or not Mil., to tempt, thugs-dám-gyi nyams sád-pa (thugs dam gyi nyams sad pa) v. above. 2. to mock, scoff, trouble maliciously, provoke, irritate C. Comp. nyams-dgú (nyams dgu) v. nyams-thábs (nyams thabs). — nyams-rgyúd (nyams rgyud) Mil. = nyams (nyams), nyams-rgyúd-la sbyáṅs-pa (nyams rgyud la sbyangs pa), intellectually skilled, well versed. — nyams-ṅá (nyams nga) anxiety, fear, dread, of a thing, with the dat. or instr. Mil.; nyams-ṅá-las thár-ba (nyams nga las thar ba) to be delivered from anxiety S.g.; nyams-ṅá-ba (nyams nga ba) vb. to be alarmed, to be in great anxiety Sch.; adj. dreadful, horrible, nags-tshál nyams-ṅa-ba (nags tshal nyams nga ba) a horrible forest Dzl. — nyams-bcág (nyams bcag) is said to be used resp. or euphem. for skyon (skyon), e.g. for damage done to an image of a god by water C.; nyams-čágs (nyams chags) sin Sch.; in Thgr. it seems to be used in this sense. — nyáms-čúṅ (nyams chung) 1. faint, weak, languid, exhausted, by hunger, illness etc. Dzl.; poor in learning, destitute of knowledge, ignorant W.; destitute of money, destitute of virtue C. 2. W. col. for snyems-čúṅ (snyems chung). — nyams-rtógs (nyams rtogs) resp. knowledge, cognition, perception, nyams-rtógs śig yod (nyams rtogs shig yod), nyams-rtógs bzáṅ-po skye (nyams rtogs bzang po skye) or ´khruṅs ('khrungs), a perception, a good thought arises (in my mind); in a general sense: nyams-rtógs-kyi mthar phyin-pa (nyams rtogs kyi mthar phyin pa) to obtain perfect knowledge Mil., frq. — nyams-stóbs (nyams stobs) strength, zin (zin) is gone Med. — nyams-stoṅ-ysál (nyams stong ysal) v. ysál-po (ysal po). — nyams-brtás byed-pa (nyams brtas byed pa) strengthening, restorative, nourishing Med., (but nyams-brtas (nyams brtas) he recovered, grew well, got up again Dzl.) — nyams-thág-pa (nyams thag pa) suffering, tormented, exhausted Dzl.; nyams-thág-pai skad (nyams thag pai skad) or sgra (sgra) lamentation, doleful cries. — nyams-thábs (nyams thabs), nyams-dgú (nyams dgu) Sch.: 'appearance, colour, figure, state' (?). — nyams-myóṅ (nyams myong) Tar. enjoyment, delight, nyams-myóṅ ma skyes ruṅ (nyams myong ma skyes rung), although I had no real enjoyment of it Mil. nt.; tsór-bai nyams-myóṅ (tsor bai nyams myong) prob. perception by the senses, knowledge acquired through the medium of the senses Mil. — nyams-rtsál (nyams rtsal) Dzl. 74, 7 skill. — nyams-mtshár-ba (nyams mtshar ba) C. wonderful, most beautiful. — nyams-lén (nyams len) a memorial verse, a rhyme or verse serving to retain things in memory Mil.

injured, hurt, e.g., by a fall Dzl.; of lifeless things: spoiled, damaged C.; impaired, imperfect, stobs-nyams (stobs nyams), dbáṅ-po nyáms-pa (dbang po nyams pa), yáṅ-lag nyams (yang lag nyams) Lex. (as explanation of żá-bo (zha bo)); smra-nyáms (smra nyams) (the sick person) speaks little Med.; *sem-nyám soṅ-kán* (*sem nyam song kan*) W. discouraged, disheartened; esp. relative to a violation of duty, failing in, tshúl-khrims (tshul khrims) (or tshúl-las (tshul las)) nyáms-pas (nyams pas) because he has failed in, acted against the moral law Dzl.; bzód-pa nyáms-par gyúr-bas (bzod pa nyams par gyur bas) because their patience failed Dzl.; also stained Glr., e.g. khrág-gis (khrag gis) with blood; nyáms-par byéd-pa (nyams par byed pa) Wdn.; nyáms-su ´júg-pa (nyams su 'jug pa) Glr. to spoil, deteriorate, destroy; ma nyáms-pa (ma nyams pa) entire, complete, untouched, uncorrupted.
1. v. nya-ra (nya ra). — 2. Cs., also nyur-nyár (nyur nyar), oblong.
W. inst. of ṅar-gdóṅ (ngar gdong) shin, shin-bone.
or nyal-nyól (nyal nyol) filth, dirt, foul matter, loose and dry dirt that may be removed by sweeping Pth., Dzl.

imp. nyol (nyol), 1. to lie down, e.g. before a tigress Dzl.; to lie down, to sleep, nyal(-du) soṅ (nyal du song) (he) went to bed Glr.; rgya-sráṅ-la nyal dúg-go (rgya srang la nyal dug go) (he) slept in the street Glr.; mi nyal tsám-la (mi nyal tsam la) when people go to bed, at curfew Mil.; rta nyal byéd-pa (rta nyal byed pa) to make a horse lie down Glr.; rarely of things: rtsva nyal (rtswa nyal) the grass is laid-down (by the wind or rain) Dzl.; ra ´og nyál-bai nya so (ra 'og nyal bai nya so) Zam. calls the letter rnya (rnya); fig. to rest, bdé-bar nyál-du méd-do (bde bar nyal du med do) (he) had no rest, viz. from envy Dzl. 72, 12. — 2. with daṅ (dang) or la (la), to lie with (a woman) Dzl. and elsewh. — 3. fig. to dwell, to live Mil. Comp. nyal-khrí (nyal khri) couch, bed, sofa C. — nyal-gós (nyal gos) counterpane, quilt, blanket Sch. — nyál-po (nyal po) coition, nyál-po byéd-pa (nyal po byed pa) to practise cohabitation, máṅ-du (mang du) immoderately Med. —
